reasoning: The statistical trend tool shows a slight downward trajectory in the recent month with a combined P(increase) estimate of 0.41 for a 1-week horizon, but weekly stock movements are largely a random walk. Utility stocks tend to range bound with mild upward drift, though quarterly ex-dividend dates (late-August, late-November) will mechanically lower the price by ~$0.67 each time over the longer intervals. I am setting the 7-day forecast slightly below 0.5 (0.48) to reflect the mild negative recent momentum, and progressively relaxing toward 0.50 for the longer horizons, since the long-term upward drift broadly offsets the ex-dividend drags and noise overtakes the immediate trend.
